title: Palantir Q4 2025 Earnings Beat Wall Street Expectations
text: Palantir Technologies surpassed analyst forecasts in its fourth quarter results, with stock jumping 10% following the announcement. The company delivered revenue of $1.4 billion, exceeding the $1.3 billion consensus estimate, representing 70% year-over-year growth. Adjusted earnings per share reached $0.25, surpassing the projected $0.23. Forward guidance also impressed investors with first quarter revenue guidance of $1.5 billion versus the $1.3 billion expected, while full-year outlook reached approximately $7.2 billion compared to analyst estimates of $6.3 billion. The standout performance came from U.S. operations. Commercial revenue nearly doubled to $507 million, outpacing the $479 million projection. Government revenue climbed 66% to $570 million, exceeding the $522 million estimate. CEO Alex Karp stated: the products and the culture we have are ideally built for the time we are in now. The earnings beat provided relief following a challenging month where shares declined roughly 12% amid broader software sector weakness and valuation concerns. William Blair analyst Louis DiPalma upgraded the stock to Outperform, citing improved valuations following the recent drawdown.
